question.text: Rajesh, a certified investment adviser, explains to his client Meera that estate planning is a comprehensive process. Which of the following BEST describes the scope of estate planning?

answer.text: It deals only with distribution of assets after death
It is relevant only for high-net-worth individuals with estates above INR 5 crore
It is the process dealing with accumulation, conservation, and distribution of an estate
It focuses exclusively on tax planning for inherited wealth

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Estate planning encompasses three key aspects — accumulation, conservation, and distribution of an estate. It is NOT limited to only distribution or only for wealthy individuals. **The Trap:** Many candidates assume estate planning is only about distributing assets after death, but it also covers accumulation and conservation during lifetime. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A (Mutual Fund) also tests basics of financial planning which includes estate planning as a component. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always educate clients that estate planning is for everyone, not just the wealthy. A middle-class family with a house, insurance, and mutual fund SIPs equally needs an estate plan.
